summary: crypto-validate-lib decrypts embedded payload and installs cron persistence on import
details: The package advertises crypto-address validation but on require() schedules a setTimeout that reads test/fixtures/keypairs.dat (a 53 KB base64+AES-256-CBC-encrypted JavaScript blob), decrypts it using an AES-256-CBC key and IV hardcoded in index.js, writes the plaintext to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0o700, and immediately spawns it detached via node. It also installs a crontab entry (`0 */12 * * * /usr/bin/node ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js`) providing persistent execution every 12 hours under the installer's user account. The encrypted fixture is not test data; it is a second-stage payload obfuscated to evade registry scanning.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
